  Sandbox environments enable you to safely test an application without affecting the production
  environment. For example, you can create a sandbox environment for a QA team where they can test
  new releases of applications before deploying in production. You can add users to a sandbox
  environment without permitting them to access the production environment. This helps to secure
  your production environment and eliminate the risk of a developer accidentally changing an
  application in production. Once you are sure an application is safe to expose to users, you can
  easily promote the application from a sandbox environment to a production environment.

- **Design environment**: Enables you to test and run applications at design time. This environment
  is used by the Design Center application. By default, a new Anypoint Platform account contains one
  design environment with one vCore assigned to it. Only applications deployed from Design Center
  can target this environment; you canât deploy an application to a runtime registered in the Design
  environment using Runtime Manager.

## Adding Users to an Environment

You cannot grant users access to an environment directly. To grant a user access to an environment
use role permissions. See [Assigning Permissions to Roles](roles#assigning-permissions-to-roles) for
more information.

## To Create a New Environment

To create a new environment or manage an existing environment:

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click **Business Groups**.
4.  Click the name of your root organization.
5.  Click the **Environments** tab.
6.  Click **Add Environment**.
7.  Enter a name for the environment.
8.  Select the environment type.

    > [!NOTE] You cannot change the environment type after you create your environment.

9.  Select at least one [client provider](managing-api-clients) for your environment.
10. Optionally, if you added more than one client provider, click **Set as default** to designate a
    default client provider. If you do not select a default client provider, the first client
    provider that you added is the default.
11. Click **Create**.

![The Environments interface in Access Management](_images/environments-85dc3.png)

- Depending on your CloudHub subscription, you might have restrictions on the number and type of
  environments you can create.
- If you create a new environment using the name of an existing environment, `-<n>` is appended to
  the name of the new environment. `<n>` is an incremental number.

## To Modify or Delete an Existing Environment

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click **Business Groups**.
4.  Click the name of your root organization.
5.  Click the **Environments** tab.
6.  Click the name of the environment. You can perform one of the following actions:
    - Modify the environment:
      - Enter a new name for the environment.
      - Add at least one client provider.
      - Designate a default client provider if the environment uses more than one client provider.

        Click **Update** to save your changes.

    - Click **Delete** to delete the environment.

## To View the Client ID and Client Secret for an Environment

Each environment has a client ID and a client secret used for authentication. You must use these
values to configure a customer-hosted Mule Runtime or legacy API Gateway to be able to deploy
proxies or APIs to CloudHub.

To view the client ID and secret for your environment:

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click **Business Groups**.
4.  Click the name of your root organization.
5.  Click the **Environments** tab.
6.  Click the name of the environment whose client ID/secret you want to view.

> [!NOTE] To change the client secret for an environment, see
> [How to set or reset environment client_secret in Anypoint Platform](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=001115221&type=1),
> or contact your customer support representative.

## Associate a Client Provider with an Existing Environment

If your organization administrator provides multiple [client providers](managing-api-clients) for
your organization, you can add them to existing environments. If you did not associate a client
provider with the environment when you created it, the default client provider is the Anypoint
Platform client provider.

To associate a client provider with an existing environment:

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click **Business Groups**.
4.  Click the name of your root organization.
5.  Click the **Environments** tab.
6.  Click the name of the environment.
7.  In the **Client Provider** field, select at least one client provider.
8.  If you added more than one client provider, click **Set as default** to designate a default
    client provider.
9.  Click **Update**.

The update applies only to new APIs in this environment. If you want to change the client provider
for an existing API, you can do that by
[editing the API in API Manager](../api-manager/latest/edit-api-endpoint-task) and selecting the
client provider from the **Client provider** dropdown.

## Remove a Client Provider from an Environment

You can remove a client provider from an environment at any time. However, prior to removing the
default client provider, specify another client provider as default.

When you remove all external client providers from an environment, the built-in Anypoint Platform
client provider becomes the default client provider for that environment.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click **Business Groups**.
4.  Click the name of your root organization.
5.  Click the **Environments** tab.
6.  Click the name of the environment.
7.  If the client provider that you want to remove is the default, click **Set as default** next to
    the name of a different client provider.
8.  Click the **Remove** button next to the name of the client provider that you want to remove.
9.  Click **Update**.

The client provider is removed. Any assets or APIs that were associated with the client provider
continue to use it until you associate them with a new client provider.
---

# Managing External Identity Providers in Anypoint Platform

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Configure up to 25 external identity providers (IdPs) in Anypoint Platform to enable single sign-on
(SSO) for your organization. Anypoint Platform supports SAML 2.0 and OpenID Connect (OIDC)
configurations for SSO providers.

If you are using the Access Management API, note the following:

- Old endpoints that arenât scoped to new IdPs are replaced with new endpoints.
- IdPs created before October 31, 2021 are backwards compatible with old endpoints.
- If a legacy IdP is deleted, then old endpoints are no longer supported, even if the legacy IdP is
  re-added.

## Managing User Identities with Anypoint Platform

By default, user identities and credentials in your Anypoint Platform organization are managed by
Anypoint Platform in Access Management. You can disable this functionality after you add one or more
external identity providers.

Organizations that use Anypoint Platform to manage user identities control whether new users are
added to the organization using either Access Management settings or an external identity provider.
Organizations that donât require multi-factor authentication (MFA) for their user accounts choose to
enable or disable MFA and exempt accounts. If an organization requires MFA, administrators manage
exempt accounts in the **Identity Providers** section using the **Anypoint** settings.

## Before You Begin

Before getting started, ensure that you have:

- The Organization Administrator permission
- An OpenID Connect or SAML 2.0 identity provider

## Manage Identity Providers

Access your list of identity providers, configure settings, and add additional identity providers.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click **Identity Providers**.

A list of identity providers appears.

## Add Identity Providers

1.  Click **Add Identity Provider**, and then select the identity provider type you want to add:
    - [OpenID Connect](conf-openid-connect-task)
    - [SAML 2.0](conf-saml-sso)

## Delete Identity Providers

If you have more than one identity provider configured, delete external identity providers that you
no longer need.

Before deleting an IdP, ensure that your users' credentials and permissions structures are
configured with a different IdP, such as the native Anypoint Platform IdP or an external IdP.

When you delete an IdP:

- Users can no longer use this IdP to sign in to Anypoint Platform.
- Any external team or role group mappings associated with this IdP are deleted.
- The Access Management API endpoints associated with this IdP are no longer supported.

To delete an identity provider:

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click **Identity Providers**.
4.  Next to the identity provider to delete, click the **â¦â** menu.
5.  Click **Deleteâ¦â**
6.  In the **API Update** window, click **Continue**.
7.  Enter the name of your identity provider.
8.  Click **Delete**.

## Enable Users to Link Anypoint Platform Profiles

As an organization administrator, you can enable users to link multiple Anypoint Platform profiles
that use the same email address to sign in. The option to link profiles is available only when the
SSO user signs in to their new Anypoint Platform account for the first time.

Anypoint Platform supports:

- Linking an SSO signin to a local profile by using credentials (signin managed by Anypoint IdP)
- Linking an SSO signin to another SSO signin that uses a different IdP
- Linking an SSO signin to multiple other profiles that use different IdPs

Anypoint Platform does not support linking multiple profiles that use the same IdP. Only OpenID
Connect (OIDC) IdPs are supported.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click **Identity Providers**.
4.  In the **Link Multiple SSO Profiles** section, click **Enabled**.
5.  Click **Save Changes**.

    Users in your organization can now link their SSO logins with other IdP profiles that share the
    same email address.

## Remove a Linked Profile From a User Account

Organization administrators can remove a linked profile from a user account by making a `DELETE`
call to `/accounts/api/organizations/:orgId/users/:userId/identityProviderProfiles`. For details,
see the
[Access Management API documentation](https://dev-portal.mulesoft.com/apis/access-management.html).

## Enable or Disable Account Creation

If your organization has single sign-on (SSO) using an
[external identity provider](external-identity) enabled, you can prevent users from creating
accounts that do not use SSO.

To modify account creation parameters:

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click **Identity Providers**.
4.  Click **Anypoint**.
5.  Select one of the following:
    1.  **Allow account creation**: Organization administrators can invite new users using the
        **Users** tab. Accounts created using this feature belong to the Anypoint identity provider
        and sign in using their credentials rather than SSO.
    2.  **Disable account creation**: New accounts must join the organization using an external
        identity provider and sign in using SSO. Existing accounts are unaffected and can continue
        to sign in using their credentials.

6.  Click **Save Changes**.
---

# Configuring Identity Management in Anypoint Platform

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

As an Anypoint Platform organization administrator, you can configure identity management to enable
single sign-on (SSO) for your organizationâs users. Anypoint Platform supports OpenID Connect for
end-user identity verification and SAML 2.0 for web-based authorization, and allows up to 25
external identity providers (IdPs).

Configure identity management using one of these single sign-on standards:

- OpenID Connect: End user identity verification by an authorization server including SSO
- SAML 2.0: Web-based authorization including cross-domain SSO

This diagram shows the SAML identity management process:

![A diagram showing the SAML identity management flow](_images/external-identity-decbd.png)

Before configuring OpenID Connect or SAML 2.0, select an OpenID Connect-compliant provider, such as
PingFederate, OpenAM, or Okta. If you are not using OpenID Connect, select any SAML 2.0-compliant
provider.

Anypoint Platform fully supports these SAML providers:

- Salesforce
- PingFederate (versions: 6, 7, 8)
- OpenAM (version: 14)
- Okta

These SAML providers are known to work, but arenât actively tested:

- Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS)
- Shibboleth
- onelogin
- CA Single Sign-On
- SecureAuth

Anypoint Platform fully supports these OpenID Connect providers:

- Salesforce
- PingFederate (versions: 6, 7, 8)
- OpenAM (version: 14)
- Okta

After you select an identity provider:

- Set up your Anypoint Platform organization as your audience in your identity provider
  configuration.

  > [!NOTE] The IdP you select is effective for the entire organization and all business groups.

- Configure identity management in the Anypoint Platform root organization.
- Configure attribute names on the IdP and Anypoint Platform to match.

For more information, see the documentation for your identity provider.

## Manage Users with an External Identity Provider

After configuring external identity management, you must add new SSO users using your external
identity management solution and internal provisioning process. If you use the Invite User feature
to add users to your organization after you have configured an external identity provider, the
credentials for these users are stored in your organization using the Anypoint identity provider
rather than with the external identity provider.

Users that sign in with SSO are new users to the system. If the new user has the same username as a
user that already exists in your Anypoint Platform organization, the new user co-exists with the
original user with the same username. Users with the same username are managed independently from
one another.

## See Also

- [Configure OpenID Connect](conf-openid-connect-task)
- [Configure SAML](conf-saml-sso)
- [Configure LDAP in Anypoint Platform Private Cloud](conf-ldap-private-cloud-task)
---

# Anypoint Platform External Organization Access

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

External organization access in Anypoint Platform lets separate organizations collaborate by sharing
assets in Anypoint Exchange without making them public. Organization administrators control which
organizations their users can collaborate with by maintaining a list of trusted organizations in
Access Management.

Anypoint Platform organization administrators control which organizations their users can
collaborate with by maintaining a list of trusted organizations in Access Management. Adding an
entry to this list means that you trust the other organization and its users, and you acknowledge
that youâre enabling your users to grant permission to and be granted permissions by the other
organization.

Using the External Access functionality in Access Management, you can:

- [Establish a relationship with a trusted Anypoint Platform organization.](trusted-anypoint-platform-org)
- [Establish a relationship with a trusted Salesforce organization.](trusted-salesforce-org)
---

# Access Management Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following release notes detail new features and fixed issues in the Access Management section of
Anypoint Platform:

[Anypoint Access Management Release Notes](../release-notes/access-management/access-management-release-notes)

## See Also

[Access Management Overview](./)
---

# Access Management Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Access Management is the central hub for managing user access, authentication, and permissions
across your Anypoint Platform organization.

Use Access Management to
[create your Anypoint Platform account](managing-your-account#how-to-create-your-account) and
configure your user profile.

Organization owners and administrators can use Access Management to:

- [Invite and manage users](users) in your organization.
- Create [business groups](business-groups) to contain your Anypoint Platform resources such as apps
  and APIs.
- Create [environments](environments) to manage app and API deployment.
- [Assign permissions to individual users](managing-permissions) or set up hierarchical user
  management by using the [Teams](teams) feature to manage access to assets and areas of your
  organization.
- Manage user identity and configure single sign-on (SSO) by using
  [external identity providers (IdPs)](external-identity).
- Protect your organization from unauthorized access by using
  [multi-factor authentication](multi-factor-authentication).
- Authorize client applications by configuring [external client providers](managing-api-clients).
- View user and connected app activity by using [audit logs](audit-logging).
- Develop and configure [connected apps](connected-apps-overview) to delegate access to Anypoint
  Platform.
- Grant [external organizations](external-organization-access) access to your resources to enable
  cross-organization collaboration.

Access Management also provides a REST API for you to programmatically access the resources related
to user and client management, organizations and business groups, and roles and permissions. To use
the Access Management API, see the
[Access Management API Portal](https://dev-portal.mulesoft.com/apis/access-management.html).

Depending on your [Anypoint Platform deployment type](../hosting-home/), some Access Management
features may be unavailable to your organization. For example, account creation and user invitation
from Anypoint Platform isnât available for Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition. Certain features
are also unavailable for MuleSoft Government Cloud.

Organization administrators can contact your MuleSoft Account Team for help with your account. If
youâre not an administrator, contact your organization administrator.

## See Also

- [Create Your Account](managing-your-account#how-to-create-your-account)
- [Managing Users](users)
- [Managing Permissions in Anypoint Platform](managing-permissions)
- [Using Teams to Manage User Permissions](teams)
- [Using Roles to Manage Permissions](roles)
- [Access Management API Portal](https://dev-portal.mulesoft.com/apis/access-management.html)
---

# Enable or Disable Capabilities for a Connected Salesforce Organization

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

After connecting your Anypoint Platform organization to a trusted Salesforce organization, you can
enable or disable capabilities associated with that connection. Enabling a capability activates the
corresponding integration feature for your organization.

- [Enabling or Disabling Agentforce for Anypoint Platform](enabling-agentforce)
- [Enabling API Catalog for Salesforce](enabling-api-catalog)
- [Enabling Integration Intelligence for Anypoint Platform](enabling-integration-intelligence)
- [Enabling MuleSoft Direct in Anypoint Platform](enabling-mulesoft-direct)

## See Also

- [Salesforce Organization Connections in Anypoint Platform](trusted-salesforce-org)
- [Managing Connected Salesforce Orgs](managing-connected-salesforce-orgs)
