policies</p></td><td><div><div><p>Edit policy configurations and add policies.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>These permissions:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>API Manager: Manage Policies</p></li><li><p>API Manager: View APIs Configuration</p></li><li><p>API Manager: View Policies</p></li><li><p>Anypoint Monitoring: Monitoring Viewer</p></li><li><p>Exchange: Exchange Viewer</p></li><li><p>Anypoint Code Builder: Mule Developer Generative AI User</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Use the <strong>Cost Management</strong> experience</p></td><td><div><div><p>Apply optimization strategies using policies, view policies, view Exchange assets, and query metrics. The Mule Developer Generative AI User permission is required to enable AI features.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Anypoint Code Builder: Mule Developer Generative AI User</p></div></div></td><td><p>Send prompts to agent</p></td><td><div><div><p>Use the native agent.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Any of these permissions:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>API Governance: Governance Administrator</p></li><li><p>API Manager: Manage Policies</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Create governance strategies</p></td><td><div><div><p>Create governance strategies.</p></div><div><p>To view and create governance strategies, you need the Governance Administrator permission for <strong>Control</strong> types, or the Manage Policies permission for <strong>Automated Policy</strong> types.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Any of these permissions:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>API Governance: Governance Viewer</p></li><li><p>API Governance: Governance Administrator</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>View governance reports</p></td><td><div><div><p>View governance reports at the service and governance strategy levels.</p></div><div><p>To see the conformance badge and whether rulesets passed or failed, you need the Exchange Viewer permission. To view or export detailed governance reports, including violations, warnings, and rule breakdowns, you need the Governance Viewer permission.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Any of these permissions:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>API Manager: View API Alerts</p></li><li><p>Runtime Manager: Read Alerts</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>View alerts</p></td><td><div><div><p>View API alerts in API Manager and read alerts in Runtime Manager.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Any of these permissions:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>API Manager: Manage API Alerts</p></li><li><p>Runtime Manager: Manage Alerts</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Manage alerts</p></td><td><div><div><p>Manage API alerts in API Manager and alerts in Runtime Manager.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Exchange

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Required Permissions</th><th>Grants the Ability to</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Exchange Administrator</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>View, create, and download assets within a business group.</p></li><li><p>Edit asset portal content in an existing asset version.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Users with this permission have the same access as users with the Exchange contributor and Exchange viewer permissions, and access to share an asset with another user, deprecate an asset, and delete an asset.</p></div><div><p>Use this permission to set up Exchange administrators for all assets within a specific business group.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Exchange Contributor</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View, create, and download assets within a business group.</p></div><div><p>Users with this permission can edit asset portal content in an existing asset version.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Use this permission to invite users to edit and maintain your asset portal descriptions.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Exchange Viewer</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View and download assets within a business group.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Users with this permission canât add new assets, edit asset portal content, or share an asset with another user.</p></div><div><p>Assign this permission to those who consume your assets in a specific business group.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Exchange Creator</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Create new assets within a business groupâs catalog.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A user with this permission canât modify assets or asset versions created by other users in the business group.</p></div><div><p>Once the users with this permission create an asset, the Asset Administrator permission is automatically assigned for the assets they create. The Asset Administrator permission allows these users to modify only the assets that they create.</p></div><div><p>Use this permission to restrict the modification of assets except for assets created by this user while allowing all developers across all teams in a business group to create new assets in Exchange.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Asset Viewer</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View and download an asset.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Users with this permission canât edit asset portal content or share an asset with another user.</p></div><div><p>Use this permission to invite a user outside your business group to view and download an asset.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Asset Contributor</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View, add a new version, and download an asset.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Use this permission to invite a user outside of your business group to view, download, and add edit portal content for an asset.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Asset Administrator</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View, create, download, deprecate, and delete an asset.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Users with this permission have the same access as users with the Exchange Administrator permission, but on only a single asset. This permission is assigned by default to an asset creator.</p></div><div><p>Use this permission to extend administrator permissions for an asset to an additional user.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Generate Asset Documentation with AI</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Generates documentation for existing asset versions within a business group using generative AI features.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## IDP

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Required Permissions</th><th>Grants the Ability to</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Manage Actions</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Complete access to IDP and assigns reviewer permission by default for every document action.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Build Actions</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Create, edit, and publish document actions and assign reviewers to the actions.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Execute Published Actions</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Execute a published document action and retrieve the results of the execution.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Configure Connected Apps</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure a connected app to communicate with IDP.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

app.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>View destinations</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>View all destinations and each destinationâs settings (<strong>ID</strong>, <strong>Type</strong>, <strong>Message TTL</strong>, and <strong>Message Lock Default TTL</strong>).</p></li><li><p>View <strong>In Queue</strong> message stats.</p></li><li><p>View <strong>In Flight</strong> message stats.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Clear destinations</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>View all destinations and each destinationâs settings (<strong>ID</strong>, <strong>Type</strong>, <strong>Message TTL</strong>, and <strong>Message Lock Default TTL</strong>).</p></li><li><p>View <strong>In Queue</strong> message stats.</p></li><li><p>View <strong>In Flight</strong> message stats.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Manage clients</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>View all client apps, including client app IDs and client secrets for each client app.</p></li><li><p>Create client apps.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Administer destinations</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>View all destinations and each destinationâs settings (<strong>ID</strong>, <strong>Type</strong>, <strong>Message TTL</strong>, and <strong>Message Lock Default TTL</strong>).</p></li><li><p>View <strong>In Queue</strong> message stats.</p></li><li><p>View <strong>In Flight</strong> message stats.</p><div><ul><li><p>Create new queues, message exchanges, and bindings.</p></li><li><p>Edit existing queues, message exchanges, and bindings.</p></li><li><p>Purge messages from queues.</p></li></ul></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Manage destinations (deprecated)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>View all destinations and each destinationâs settings (<strong>ID</strong>, <strong>Type</strong>, <strong>Message TTL</strong>, and <strong>Message Lock Default TTL</strong>).</p></li><li><p>View <strong>In Queue</strong> message stats.</p></li><li><p>View <strong>In Flight</strong> message stats.</p></li><li><p>Create new queues and message exchanges.</p></li><li><p>Edit existing queues and message exchange settings.</p></li><li><p>Access <strong>Message Sender</strong> and <strong>Browser</strong> pages.</p></li><li><p>Delete messages.</p></li><li><p>Purge messages from queues.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This permission is deprecated. Assign these permissions instead:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Administer destinations</p></li><li><p>Destination subscriber for given environment</p></li><li><p>Destination publisher for given environment</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Destination subscriber for given environment</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Consume messages from a destination.</p></li><li><p>Delete messages from a destination.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Destination publisher for given environment</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Send messages to a destination.</p></li><li><p>Update message TTL on a destination.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Read MQ Stats</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View organization and environment statistics.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Partner Manager

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Required Permissions</th><th>Grants the Ability to</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Partner Manager Administrator</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Have complete access to the host, partner, message flow configurations, and transaction activity.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>View Host, Partners and Message Flows</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Have view-only access to the host, partner, and message flow configurations.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This user canât view transaction activity.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Manage Partners and Message Flows</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Create, modify, and delete partners or message flow configurations.</p></li><li><p>View partner configurations.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This user canât view and manage transaction activity.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Manage Activity</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View and manage transaction activity.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This user canât view or modify either partner or message flow configurations.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Manage Host</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Create, modify, and delete host configurations.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This user canât view or modify partner configurations or transaction activity. This access applies even if the user has the Organization Administrator permission.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>View Activity</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Have view-only access to transaction activity.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This user canât view or modify either partner or message flow configurations.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## RPA

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Required Permissions</th><th>Grants the Ability to</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Administrator</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Perform all tasks, except those provided by the RPA Project Manager permission.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A user with this permission can only view or administer automation projects if the user is part of the process team.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Automations Designer</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>RPA developer, citizen technologist, or knowledge source (such as a business analyst or process owner):</p></div><div><p>In RPA Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Create automation projects.</p></li><li><p>Record or design models of business processes that a process manager or center of excellence approved for automation.</p></li><li><p>Document and edit the applications required for performing the processes.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><details><summary>Replaces these permissions</summary><div><div><ul><li><p>Application Create</p></li><li><p>Application Edit</p></li><li><p>Process Automation Open</p></li><li><p>Process Create</p></li><li><p>Process Recording</p></li></ul></div></div></details></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Automations Contributor</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>RPA developer, citizen technologist, or knowledge source (such as a business analyst or process owner):</p></div><div><ul><li><p>In RPA Manager:</p><div><ul><li><p>Create automation projects.</p></li><li><p>Record or design models of business processes that a process manager or center of excellence approved for automation.</p></li><li><p>Document and edit the applications required for performing the processes.</p></li><li><p>Create and edit global variables to link to activity parameters created with RPA Builder.</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>In RPA Builder:</p><div><ul><li><p>Build the automation based on the model.</p></li><li><p>Reuse activities from the Activity Library.</p></li></ul></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><details><summary>Replaces these permissions</summary><div><div><ul><li><p>Activity Library Open</p></li><li><p>Application Create</p></li><li><p>Application Edit</p></li><li><p>Builder Usage</p></li><li><p>Global Variables Create for Productionphase</p></li><li><p>Global Variables Create for Testphase</p></li><li><p>Global Variables Edit for Productionphase</p></li><li><p>Global Variables Edit for Testphase</p></li><li><p>Process Automation Open</p></li><li><p>Process Create</p></li><li><p>Process Recording</p></li></ul></div></div></details></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Automations Manager</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>RPA developer, citizen technologist, or knowledge source (such as a business analyst or process owner):</p></div><div><ul><li><p>In RPA Manager:</p><div><ul><li><p>Create automation projects.</p></li><li><p>Record or design models of business processes that a process manager or center of excellence approved for automation.</p></li><li><p>Document and manage the applications required for performing the processes.</p></li><li><p>Create and manage global variables to link to activity parameters created with RPA Builder.</p></li><li><p>Change the owners and managers of processes.</p></li><li><p>Reassign unprocessed user tasks</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>In RPA Builder:</p><div><ul><li><p>Build the automation based on the model.</p></li><li><p>Reuse and manage activities from the Activity Library.</p></li></ul></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><details><summary>Replaces these permissions</summary><div><div><ul><li><p>Activity Library Administration</p></li><li><p>Activity Library Open</p></li><li><p>Application Create</p></li><li><p>Application Delete</p></li><li><p>Application Edit</p></li><li><p>Builder Usage</p></li><li><p>Change Process Owner</p></li><li><p>Change Project Manager</p></li><li><p>Global Variables Create for Productionphase</p></li><li><p>Global Variables Create for Testphase</p></li><li><p>Global Variables Edit for Productionphase</p></li><li><p>Global Variables Edit for Testphase</p></li><li><p>Global Variables Delete</p></li><li><p>Process Automation Open</p></li><li><p>Process Create</p></li><li><p>Process Recording</p></li><li><p>Unprocessed Task List Edit</p></li><li><p>Unprocessed Task List Open</p></li></ul></div></div></details></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Bots Manager</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>In RPA Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Monitor and manage all RPA Bots, including:</p><div><ul><li><p>Manage service times.</p></li><li><p>View session queues.</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Manage the applications required for performing the processes, including downtimes.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><details><summary>Replaces these permissions</summary><div><div><ul><li><p>Application Create</p></li><li><p>Application Delete</p></li><li><p>Application Edit</p></li><li><p>Process Monitoring Open</p></li><li><p>Robot Management Administration</p></li><li><p>Robot Management Open</p></li><li><p>Robot State and Operation Open</p></li><li><p>Service Time Create</p></li><li><p>Service Time Delete</p></li><li><p>Service Time Edit</p></li></ul></div></div></details></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Evaluations Viewer</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>In RPA Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>View all process evaluations, regardless of whether the user belongs to the process team.</p></li><li><p>View:</p><div><ul><li><p>Evaluation criteria</p></li><li><p>Evaluation templates</p></li></ul></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><details><summary>Replaces these permissions</summary><div><div><ul><li><p>Evaluation Criteria Open</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Templates Open</p></li><li><p>Global Process Evaluation View</p></li><li><p>Process Evaluation Open</p></li></ul></div></div></details></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Evaluations Contributor</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>In RPA Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>View all process evaluations, regardless of whether the user belongs to the process team.</p></li><li><p>View, create, and edit:</p><div><ul><li><p>Evaluation criteria</p></li><li><p>Evaluation templates</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>View, create, edit, and delete process evaluations.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><details><summary>Replaces these permissions</summary><div><div><ul><li><p>Evaluation Criteria Create</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Criteria Edit</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Criteria Open</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Templates Create</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Templates Edit</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Templates Open</p></li><li><p>Global Process Evaluation View</p></li><li><p>Process Evaluation Administration</p></li><li><p>Process Evaluation Open</p></li></ul></div></div></details></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Evaluations Manager</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>In RPA Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>View all process evaluations, regardless of whether the user belongs to the process team.</p></li><li><p>View, create, edit, and delete:</p><div><ul><li><p>Evaluation criteria</p></li><li><p>Evaluation templates</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Process evaluations</p></li><li><p>Approve evaluated processes to a center of excellence or a project manager for automation.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><details><summary>Replaces these permissions</summary><div><div><ul><li><p>Evaluation Criteria Create</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Criteria Delete</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Criteria Edit</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Criteria Open</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Templates Create</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Templates Delete</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Templates Edit</p></li><li><p>Evaluation Templates Open</p></li><li><p>Global Process Evaluation View</p></li><li><p>Process Evaluation Administration</p></li><li><p>Process Evaluation Approval to a CoE</p></li><li><p>Process Evaluation Approval to a Project manager</p></li><li><p>Process Evaluation Open</p></li></ul></div></div></details></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Operations Viewer</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>In RPA Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>View all aspects of these RPA assets:</p><div><ul><li><p>Alerts</p></li><li><p>Bots</p></li><li><p>Dashboards</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>View this information for processes for which the user belongs to the process team:</p><div><ul><li><p>Deployment maps</p></li><li><p>Execution plans</p></li><li><p>Session queues</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Watch the bot at work via process streaming.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><details><summary>Replaces these permissions</summary><div><div><ul><li><p>Alerting Open</p></li><li><p>Dashboard Open</p></li><li><p>Process Deployment Map Open</p></li><li><p>Process Execution Plans Open</p></li><li><p>Process Monitoring Open</p></li><li><p>Process Streaming Open</p></li><li><p>Robot Management Open</p></li><li><p>Robot State and Operation Open</p></li></ul></div></div></details></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Operations Manager</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>In RPA Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>View and manage these RPA assets:</p><div><ul><li><p>Alerts</p></li><li><p>Bots</p></li><li><p>Dashboards</p></li><li><p>Unprocessed user tasks</p></li><li><p>Upcoming process changes</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Analyze finance aspects and billing reports</p></li><li><p>View this information for processes for which the user belongs to the process team:</p><div><ul><li><p>Deployment maps</p></li><li><p>Execution plans</p></li><li><p>Session queues</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Watch the bot at work via process streaming.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><details><summary>Replaces these permissions</summary><div><div><ul><li><p>Alerting Administration</p></li><li><p>Alerting Open</p></li><li><p>Dashboard Open</p></li><li><p>Process Deployment Map Open</p></li><li><p>Process Execution Plans Open</p></li><li><p>Process Monitoring Open</p></li><li><p>Process Streaming Open</p></li><li><p>Robot Management Open</p></li><li><p>Robot State and Operation Open</p></li><li><p>Billing Report Open</p></li><li><p>Dashboard Administration</p></li><li><p>Finance Analysis Edit</p></li><li><p>Finance Analysis Open</p></li><li><p>Global Finance Analysis View</p></li><li><p>Global Process Execution Plans View</p></li><li><p>Unprocessed Task List Edit</p></li><li><p>Unprocessed Task List Open</p></li><li><p>Upcoming Process Changes Administration</p></li><li><p>Upcoming Process Changes Open</p></li></ul></div></div></details></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Performance Analyzer</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>In RPA Manager:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Analyze the financial aspects (such as the break-even point) of all processes.</p></li><li><p>View the billing reports.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><details><summary>Replaces these permissions</summary><div><div><ul><li><p>Billing Report Open</p></li><li><p>Finance Analysis Edit</p></li><li><p>Finance Analysis Open</p></li><li><p>Global Finance Analysis View</p></li></ul></div></div></details></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>RPA Project Manager</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Be assigned as a project manager of automation projects in RPA Manager. For a user to function as a project manager, the user must also have one of these permissions:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RPA Automations Designer</p></li><li><p>RPA Automations Contributor</p></li><li><p>RPA Automations Manager</p></li><li><p>RPA Administrator</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Replaces the RPA Project Management permissions.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Runtime Manager

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Required Permissions</th><th>Grants the Ability to</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>CloudHub Network Viewer</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View CloudHub and CloudHub 2.0 network resources.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Delete Applications</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View applications in a specific environment.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Manage Servers</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Create, update, and delete server and Omni Gateway resources.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Read Servers</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View server and Omni Gateway resources.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Manage Application Flows</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Update flows.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Create Applications</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Create applications in a specific environment.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Restart Applications</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Restart applications deployed to Runtime Fabric in a specific environment.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

---

# Private Cloud Edition Features

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition enables you to:

1.  Configure [User Management](external-identity#user-management) using
    [LDAP](external-identity#configure-ldap).
2.  Configure an [SMTP server](#smtp) to send API Manager alerts from your Anypoint Platform Private
    Cloud Edition installation.
3.  Set your [certificates and private key](#security) to handle SSL encryption for Anypoint
    Platform.
4.  Add a customized [disclaimer](#disclaimer) to the Anypoint Platform Signin page.

## SMTP

Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition requires you to set up your own SMTP email server to deliver
email alerts for both **API Manager** and **Runtime manager**.

> [!NOTE] This service doesnât create an SMTP server for you. You must configure the service with
> the settings of an existing SMTP server within your environment.

1.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click the **SMTP** option and configure the details
    for your SMTP server:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th><th>Example</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Host</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The host name of your SMTP server</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>smtp.gmail.com</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Port</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The port used to communicate to your SMTP server</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>587</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Username</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The username to authenticate to your SMTP server</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>mulesoft.onprem.smtp@gmail.com</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Password</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Your SMTP password</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>examplepassphrase</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  After you enter your configuration information, click **Save**.

## DNS or IP

You must provide the DNS or IP that is exposed on your instance of the Anypoint Platform to the
local network, to enable Mule runtime to communicate with it.

1.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select the **DNS/IP** tab.
2.  In the **Platform DNS/IP** field, enter the address where you are hosting Anypoint Platform.
3.  Click **Save**.

## Security

You can configure Anypoint Platform to use your own certificate, and you can also choose the cipher
and DH groups for the key exchange method.

1.  In the Access Management navigation menu, click the **Security** link.

    ![A form with fields to upload a certificate and a private key, a checkbox to use default ciphers and DH group, and a Save button](_images/on-prem-features-5f98d.png)

2.  In the **Certificate** field, click **Browse** to locate and select your certificate.
3.  In the **Key** field, click **Browse** to locate and select the private key for the certificate
    you provided.
4.  Click **Save**.
5.  Optionally, you can disregard the default configuration and choose your own cipher and
    Diffie-Hellman group file.

    ![A form with fields for a certificate, private key, ciphers, and DH group](_images/on-premises-features-3120e.png)

6.  Deselect **Use default ciphers and DH Group**.
    1.  Enter the cipher you want to use, for example, `ECDHE-RSA-AES128-SHA256`.
    2.  Upload a DH Group.

## Set up a Disclaimer Message

1.  Click the **Disclaimer** section to set up a disclaimer message that your users see when they
    access the Anypoint Platform Signin page.

    This can also be a custom HTML message.

2.  Click **Save**.
---

# Using Roles to Manage Permissions

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

A _role_ is a set of predefined permissions controlling access to each product or feature within
Anypoint Platform. Depending on the product, you can use predefined roles with standard permissions,
or you can specify your own permissions for each role. Roles are set only for individual users. Use
the [Teams feature](teams) for an access solution that works across business groups.

To manage roles and permissions within Anypoint Platform, you must have the Organization
Administrator permission. To manage user permissions for an API version, you must have the API
Versions Owner permission.

Anypoint Platform provides two types of roles:

- **Default roles**: Roles that are created automatically when an organization or business group is
  created. These roles provide permissions and access to core functionality of Anypoint Platform.
  You can assign users to default roles, but you canât delete default roles.
- **Custom roles**: You can create and delete custom roles. You can assign users and add permissions
  to a custom role, as well as associate a custom role with specific Anypoint Platform products. For
  example, you can create a custom role called "Application Designer" and then grant Design Center
  permissions to the role so users assigned the Application Designer role can access Design Center.

Roles are business group specific, so make sure youâre in the correct business group when managing
roles by clicking the menu next to your username.

## Access Roles

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.

## Default Roles

Default roles are automatically created as part of an organization. Each default role in Anypoint
Platform contains a set of pre-defined permissions and scope of actions that enable the user to
perform specific actions within the platform.

For more information on permissions in Anypoint Platform, see
[Managing Permissions](managing-permissions).

### Default Roles and Permission Scopes

- **API Creators**: Enables a user to create and manage an API in Anypoint Platform. Members of the
  API Creator role can add new APIs to the platform on the API administration page. This role
  doesnât grant privileges for Runtime Manager. This role has the following permission scopes:
  - Create/Import an API

- **API Versions Owner**: Enables a user to manage, delete, and deprecate any API in the
  organization. They can edit the portal of any API in the organization. However, this role doesnât
  enable a user to create a new version of an existing API. This role has the following permission
  scopes:
  - Manage any API Version in the organization
  - Delete any API Version in the organization
  - Deprecate any API Version in the organization
  - Edit the Portal of any API version in the organization

- **Audit Log Viewers**: Enables a user to access the UI for the Audit Log under Access Management.
  This role has the following permission scopes:
  - Visualize logs in the [Audit Logs](audit-logging) section.

- **CloudHub Admin**: Provides access to all Runtime Manager functionality. This role has the
  following permission scopes:
  - Create Applications
  - Delete Applications
  - Download Applications
  - Manage Alerts
  - Manage Application Data
  - Manage Queues
  - Manage Schedules
  - Manage Servers
  - Manage Settings
  - Read Applications

- **CloudHub Developer**: **(Deprecated)** Provides access to all Runtime Manager functionality,
  except organization and billing settings. This role has the following permission scopes:
  - Create Applications
  - Download Applications
  - Manage Alerts
  - Manage Application Data
  - Manage Queues
  - Manage Schedules
  - Manage Settings
  - Read Applications
  - Read Servers

- **CloudHub Support**: **(Deprecated)** Provides read-only access to dashboards, notifications,
  alerts, logs, and their user settings. This role has the following permission scopes:
  - Read Applications

- **Organization Administrators**: Enables a user to edit all versions of all APIs, all registered
  applications, and all API Portals in Anypoint Platform. Access to the Organization Administration
  page, where the user can add and manage users and roles, view and edit organization details,
  access **API Manager > Client Applications**, access the client ID and client secret for the
  organization, and customize the theme of the Developer Portal. Members of the Organization
  Administrators role also inherit the role of **API Creator** by default. This role has the
  following permission scopes:
  - Edit developer portal theme settings
  - Request API access terms and conditions
  - Edit portal terms and conditions
  - Set custom policies
  - Manage access of third-party applications to an API (specific to the organization administrator
    of the root business group)
  - Edit users' email addresses
  - Grant VPC and CloudHub dedicated load balancer permissions

- **Exchange Administrators**: Enables a user to manage Exchange Portals, including customization,
  manage assets, manage reviews. This role has the following permission scopes:
  - Create content
  - Manage assets
  - Publish, delete, and deprecate content
  - Manage public visibility of assets
  - Customize Exchange portal

- **Exchange Contributors**: This role has the following permission scopes:
  - Create content
  - Manage own content, including the ability to add a new version and edit your own version
    content, but you canât delete your own version.
  - Manage own reviews, including the ability to add, edit, and delete

- **Exchange Viewers**: Enables a user to view and consume Exchange assets. This role has the
  following permission scopes:
  - View and consume Exchange assets
  - Manage own reviews, including the ability to add, edit, and delete

- **Portals Viewer**: (Deprecated in API Manager 2.0) The ability to view an API Portal doesnât
  automatically give a user access to the API. You canât grant Portal Viewer permissions unless the
  API has an API Portal. Users with this role can view API Portals from the Developer Portal,
  including private API portals where they have Portal Viewer permissions.

> [!NOTE] If youâre using API Manager 2.0, users with the Portals Viewer role arenât able to view
> private APIs. For API Manager 2.0, assign the user at least the Exchange Viewers role to allow the
> user access to all Exchange assets within the business group where you assigned the permission.  
> To control access for a single private API, donât assign the Exchange Viewers role to the user for
> the business group where the private API resides. Instead, use the share asset feature in
> Exchange. See [Share an Asset](../exchange/to-share-an-asset). Only users with the Exchange
> Administrators role can share an asset with another user.

### Add Users to a Default Role

To add a user to a role, the user must belong to the same business group as the role.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.
6.  Click the name of the default role.

    Default roles donât have a radial button next to them.

7.  Enter the userâs name in the search box.
8.  Select the user from the drop-down list of results, then click the blue plus sign on the right
    of the search box.
9.  To change the roleâs description:
    1.  Click the description.

        The description changes to an editable field.

    2.  Enter the new description for the role, then press `Enter`.

> [!NOTE] When editing role names and descriptions, use the 'Enter' key to save changes. Clicking
> outside the text box cancels the edit without saving it.

## Custom Roles

As an organization administrator, you can create custom roles by combining APIs or applications,
permissions, and users. Available options vary by product. For example, API roles canât be removed
and their permissions canât be modified, but you can add a description and assign users to the role.

> [!NOTE] Product permissions are specific to a single environment. If you have multiple
> environments and want to give a role the same permissions on all, you must add these permissions
> multiple timesââone for each environment.

### Create a Custom Role

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.
6.  Click **Add role**.
7.  Enter a **Name** and **Description** for your custom role.  
    Your custom role now appears in your list of roles.
8.  Click the name of your new role to assign permissions to it.

After creating a custom role, you can access more information about that role, change its name and
description, add permissions to it, or assign this role to specific users.

> [!NOTE] When editing role names and descriptions, use the 'Enter' key to save changes. Clicking
> outside the text box cancels the edit without saving it.

### Add a User to a Custom Role

After creating a custom role, you can assign users to the role.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.
6.  Click the name of the custom role to add users to.
7.  Click the **Users** tab, then enter a username or email in the search field.
8.  Select the user, then click the **+** icon.

### Assign Permission Scopes to a Custom Role

After creating a custom role, you can assign permissions to the role. Some permissions within a role
can be applied to specific environments. If these environments belong to a business group rather
than the root organization, theyâre available only when creating a role in that same business group.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.
6.  Click the name of the custom role to add permission scopes to.
7.  Select the **Permissions** tab, and click **Add Permissions**.
8.  Select the permissions to add and click **Next**.

    The list of **Permissions** includes all permissions, even if they are already assigned to
    users. If you add a permission thatâs already assigned, there is no effect.

9.  Select which environments to apply the permission to if itâs enabled for the permission.
10. Click **Next**.
11. Review the permissions and environments they apply to, and then click **Add Permissions**.

    The permissions you selected now apply to the members of the role and appear in the
    **Permissions** section for the role.

### Enable Access to Design Center

After creating a custom role, grant the Design Center Developer, Design Center Viewer, or Design
Center Creator permission scope to the custom role to enable access to Design Center.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.
6.  Click the name of the custom role you want to add users to.
7.  Click **Permissions**, then click **Add permissions**.
8.  In the Design Center section, next to each permission you want to grant the user, select the
    checkbox.
9.  Click **Next** and then click **Add permissions** to add the permission.

### Assign API Permissions to a Custom Role

#### API Manager 1.x

You can associate an API to a custom role. This enables you to create roles that give users access
to a specific API.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.
6.  Click the name of the custom role to grant API access to.

    Custom roles have a radial button next to them.

7.  Click **Permissions**, then click **APIs**.
8.  Select an API from the drop-down list.
9.  Select the API version.  
    Select **All** to enable access to all versions of this API.
10. Select the permission from the drop-down list. You can add the following permissions to an API:
    - API Version Owner
    - Portal Viewer

### API Manager 2.x

You can associate environment permissions to the custom role. This enables you to create roles that
give users different permissions over environments.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.
6.  Click the name of the custom role to grant API access to.

    Custom roles have a radial button next to them.

7.  Click **Permissions**, then click **API Manager**.
8.  Select an environment from the drop-down list.
9.  Select the permission from the drop-down list. You can add the following permissions to an
    environment:
    - API Manager Environment Administrator
    - Deploy API Proxy
    - Manage API Alerts
    - Manage APIs Configuration
    - Manage Contracts
    - Manage Policies
    - View API Alerts
    - View APIs Configuration
    - View Contracts
    - View Policies

## Role Mapping

You can set up your Anypoint Platform organization so that when a SAML user belongs to certain
groups, Anypoint Platform automatically grants certain equivalent roles in your Anypoint Platform
organization.

## Migrate Roles to Teams

After you opt in to the Teams feature, you can convert existing roles into permissions, convert
roles to teams, or merge roles with teams.

When you convert or merge a role, the role is deleted.

### Convert a Role to Permissions

When you convert roles to permissions, users keep the same permissions that they previously received
with roles.

> [!WARNING] When you convert a role to permissions, the users who previously belonged to the role
> are no longer grouped according to their permissions and must be managed individually.
> Additionally, roles that can now be applied as (or converted to) permissions, such as the Exchange
> Contributors permission, can be managed only while youâre using the Teams feature.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.
6.  Next to the role that you want to convert, click the **â¦â** menu.
7.  Click **Convert to permissionsâ¦â**.
8.  Click **Convert to Permissions**.

    The users who previously had the role are granted the same permissions that the role granted
    them, and the role is deleted.

### Convert a Role to a Team

When you convert a role to a team, you create a new team that contains the users who previously held
the converted role. The team you create also inherits permissions from the parent team you create it
under.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.
6.  Next to the role that you want to convert, click the **â¦â** menu.
7.  Click **Convert to teamâ¦â**.
8.  Enter a name for your new team.  
    You can use alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and spaces in your team name.
9.  Select a parent team.  
    The team youâre creating also inherits permissions from the parent team you select.
10. Click **Convert to Team**.

    The users who previously had the role are added to a new team that has the same permissions as
    those granted by the role, and the role is deleted. Any external names formerly associated with
    the role are associated with the new team.

### Merge a Role with a Team

When you merge a role with a team, the users who previously had the role keep the permissions
granted by that role and also obtain permissions included in the team they have joined.

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Organization Administrator
    permission.
2.  Select **Access Management** from the ![gear icon](_images/gear.svg) menu.
3.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
4.  Select the name of the organization to access.
5.  Select the **Roles** tab.
6.  Next to the role that you want to convert, click the **â¦â** menu.
7.  Click **Merge with teamâ¦â**.
8.  Select the team to merge the role members with.
9.  Click **Merge with Team**.

    The users who previously had the role are added to the selected team along with the role
    permissions, and the role is deleted. Any external names formerly associated with the role are
    associated with the selected team.

## View Limits for a Role

Each role has a **Limits** section that shows how close it is to hitting limits imposed by Anypoint
Platform. This feature is available only in the new Access Management user interface.

To view limits:

1.  In the Access Management navigation menu, select **Business Groups**.
2.  Select the name of the organization to access.
3.  Select the **Roles** tab.
4.  Click the role with the limits you want to view.
5.  Click the **Limits** tab.

For more information on limits in Access Management, see
[Limits](troubleshooting-anypoint-platform-access#access-management-limits).

## See Also

- [Teams](teams)
- [Anypoint Platform Business Groups](business-groups)
- [Manage Permissions](managing-permissions)
- [Design Center](../design-center/)
- [Map Users to Roles in an LDAP Group](map-users-roles-teams)
- [Environments](environments)
